---
title: "stroma"
author: "Sherry Ali"
date: "2025-12-17"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown
rm(list = ls())

library(SingleR)
library(celldex)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(Seurat)
library(patchwork)
library(tseries)
library(data.table)
library(hdf5r)

# 1. Define the path to your .h5 file
h5_path <- "C:/Users/alial/OneDrive/Desktop/BMS_samples/HL/Targeted_NGSC3_DI_HodgkinsLymphoma_Pan_Cancer_raw_feature_bc_matrix.h5"

# 2. Read the H5 data -this returns a sparse matrix of counts
raw_counts <- Read10X_h5(filename = h5_path)

# 3. Initialize the Seurat object
HL <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = raw_counts, 
                           project = "Hodgkin's_Lymphoma_Analysis", 
                           min.cells = 3, 
                           min.features = 200)

# Check the object dimensions (Variables = Genes, Observations = Cells)
print(HL)
# THere are 5364 features across 1772 samples within 1 assay
# Active assay: RNA (5364 features, 0 variable features)

# Calculate QC Metrics - different method than in the Seurat workflow
# Identify mitochondrial genes (Human: ^MT-, Mouse: ^mt-)
mt_genes <- grep(pattern = "^MT-", x = rownames(HL), value = TRUE)

# Get the counts matrix directly
counts_matrix <- GetAssayData(HL, assay = "RNA", layer = "counts")

# Calculate percentage and add to 'HL' metadata
HL$percent.mt <- Matrix::colSums(counts_matrix[mt_genes, , drop = FALSE]) / 
                 Matrix::colSums(counts_matrix) * 100

#  Check the results - this all looks good
summary(HL$percent.mt)
head(HL@meta.data)

# Visualize QC metrics as a violin plot
# Create a 3-panel plotting area
par(mfrow = c(1, 3))

# Plot each metric manually - I kept getting a S4SXP error for the violin plot
boxplot(HL$nFeature_RNA, main = "Gene Count", col = "lightblue")
boxplot(HL$nCount_RNA, main = "UMI Count", col = "lightgreen")
boxplot(HL$percent.mt, main = "Mito %", col = "lightcoral")

plot1 <- FeatureScatter(HL, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "percent.mt")
plot2 <- FeatureScatter(HL, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "nFeature_RNA")
plot1 + plot2

HL <- subset(HL, subset = nFeature_RNA > 200 & nFeature_RNA < 1200 & percent.mt < 5)
dim(HL)
# dimensions are now 5364    1738. reduced to 1738 samples

HL <- NormalizeData(HL, normalization.method = "LogNormalize", scale.factor = 10000)

#Identification of highly variable features (feature selection)
HL <- FindVariableFeatures(HL, selection.method = "vst", nfeatures = 2000)

# Identify the 10 most highly variable genes
top10 <- head(VariableFeatures(HL), 10)

# plot variable features with and without labels
plot1 <- VariableFeaturePlot(HL)
plot2 <- LabelPoints(plot = plot1, points = top10, repel = TRUE)
plot1 + plot2

#Scale the data
all.genes <- rownames(HL)
HL <- ScaleData(HL, features = all.genes)

# Perform Linear dimensional reduction and analyze plots
HL <- RunPCA(HL, features = VariableFeatures(object = HL))
VizDimLoadings(HL, dims = 1:2, reduction = "pca")
DimPlot(HL, reduction = "pca") + NoLegend()
DimHeatmap(HL, dims = 1, cells = 500, balanced = TRUE)

# Determine the dimensionality with an elbow plot
ElbowPlot(HL)

# Cluster the cells
HL <- FindNeighbors(HL, dims = 1:10)
HL <- FindClusters(HL, resolution = 0.5)
## Number of nodes: 1738
## Number of edges: 61943
## Maximum modularity in 10 random starts: 0.8857
## Number of communities: 10

# Run non-linear dimensional reduction (UMAP/tSNE)
HL <- RunUMAP(HL, dims = 1:10)
DimPlot(HL, reduction = "umap", label=TRUE)


HL <- RunTSNE(HL, dims = 1:15)
DimPlot(HL, reduction = "tsne")

# find markers for every cluster compared to all remaining cells, report only the positive
# ones
HL.markers <- FindAllMarkers(HL, only.pos = TRUE)
HL.markers %>%
    group_by(cluster) %>%
    dplyr::filter(avg_log2FC > 1)
    
cluster0.markers <- FindMarkers(HL, ident.1 = 0)
head(cluster0.markers, n = 5)   

cluster1.markers <- FindMarkers(HL, ident.1 = 1)
head(cluster1.markers, n = 5)

cluster2.markers <- FindMarkers(HL, ident.1 = 2)
head(cluster2.markers, n = 5)

cluster3.markers <- FindMarkers(HL, ident.1 = 3)
head(cluster3.markers, n = 5)
    
cluster4.markers <- FindMarkers(HL, ident.1 = 4)
head(cluster4.markers, n = 5)

cluster5.markers <- FindMarkers(HL, ident.1 = 5)
head(cluster5.markers, n = 5)

cluster6.markers <- FindMarkers(HL, ident.1 = 6)
head(cluster6.markers, n = 5)

cluster7.markers <- FindMarkers(HL, ident.1 = 7)
head(cluster7.markers, n = 5)

cluster8.markers <- FindMarkers(HL, ident.1 = 8)
head(cluster8.markers, n = 5)

cluster9.markers <- FindMarkers(HL, ident.1 = 9)
head(cluster9.markers, n = 5)

FeaturePlot(HL, features = c("IL7R", "RPL22"), reduction = "tsne")
FeaturePlot(HL, features = c("PAX5", "HLA-A"), reduction = "tsne")
FeaturePlot(HL, features = c("CHEK1", "SHD"), reduction = "tsne")
FeaturePlot(HL, features = c("ID2", "COL3A1"), reduction = "tsne")
#Assigning cell type identity to clusters
# Load the reference database
ref_db <- HumanPrimaryCellAtlasData()
#--------------------------------------------------
# Get the normalized data from your HL object
# Using the 'data' layer is critical for accurate comparison
test_data <- GetAssayData(HL, layer = "data")

#  Run SingleR
# clusters = HL$seurat_clusters ensures it labels your specific clusters
predictions <- SingleR(test = test_data, 
                       ref = ref_db, 
                       labels = ref_db$label.main, 
                       clusters = HL$seurat_clusters)

#  View the labels
data.frame(Cluster = rownames(predictions), CellType = predictions$labels)

#map labels back to the object
#  Store the labels in the metadata
HL[["SingleR_labels"]] <- predictions$labels[match(HL$seurat_clusters, rownames(predictions))]

# Set the active identity to the new labels
Idents(HL) <- "SingleR_labels"

# Plot the t-SNE with automated names!
DimPlot(HL, reduction = "tsne", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE)
 
# Again with fine labels
predictions <- SingleR(test = test_data, 
                       ref = ref_db, 
                       labels = ref_db$label.fine, 
                       clusters = HL$seurat_clusters)

data.frame(Cluster = rownames(predictions), CellType = predictions$labels)

HL[["SingleR_labels"]] <- predictions$labels[match(HL$seurat_clusters, rownames(predictions))]


Idents(HL) <- "SingleR_labels"

DimPlot(HL, reduction = "tsne", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE)
